notes: Lockheed Martin developed the Cyber Kill Chain (2011), one of the foundational models for understanding adversary operations. Based on military kill chain concept. While MITRE ATT&CK provides detailed techniques, the Kill Chain provides strategic phases of a targeted intrusion.

data.description: The Cyber Kill Chain describes the seven phases of a targeted cyber intrusion, from initial reconnaissance through achieving objectives.
data.notes: Published 2011 by Lockheed Martin. Foundational to modern threat modeling. Enables 'defense in depth' — multiple opportunities to detect and disrupt attacks at each phase. Known limitations: assumes linear progression (real attacks skip/repeat phases), focuses on external threats, lacks technique-level detail. These limitations led to the Unified Kill Chain and MITRE ATT&CK.

data.note: No per-phase URL. The Cyber Kill Chain is a conceptual model — all phases are documented on the main page and PDF.

examples.note: Phase 1: Target research, harvesting emails, OSINT
Phase 2: Coupling exploit with backdoor into deliverable payload
Phase 3: Transmitting weapon to target (email, web, USB)
Phase 4: Triggering the exploit (vulnerability, user action)
Phase 5: Installing backdoor/implant on victim system
Phase 6: Command and control channel for remote manipulation
